macro_events.event_summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang joined a US presidential delegation to China on Air Force One, signaling potential easing of chip export restrictions and renewed access to the Chinese AI market.
primary_drivers: Potential relaxation of US export controls on advanced AI chips to China
Restoration of Nvidia's China revenue stream (historically ~20-25% of data center sales pre-restrictions)
Diplomatic signaling that US-China tech decoupling may be softening

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct beneficiary — Huang's personal presence on AF1 suggests a deal involving Nvidia's China chip sales (H20 successor or similar export-compliant variants). China was historically ~20%+ of Nvidia data center revenue before restrictions; any relaxation directly restores billions in lost sales.

Direct peer with similar China AI accelerator exposure (MI-series). Any framework allowing Nvidia chip sales to China would almost certainly extend to AMD's export-compliant SKUs. Sympathy trade plus genuine revenue optionality.
Dutch lithography supplier — eased US stance on China semis reduces pressure for further restrictions on ASML's DUV/EUV sales to Chinese fabs, a major overhang on the stock.
Applied Materials derives meaningful revenue from Chinese fab equipment sales; any thaw in US-China tech tensions reduces incremental export-control risk on WFE shipments.
Lam Research has similar China WFE exposure (~30%+ of revenue at peak); benefits from same easing-of-controls narrative as AMAT.
Micron was specifically banned from sales to Chinese critical infrastructure operators in 2023. A broader US-China tech detente raises probability of that ban being relaxed or not extended.
TSMC fabs Nvidia's chips; restored China demand for Nvidia AI accelerators directly lifts TSMC wafer volumes at leading-edge nodes.

losers.rationale: Broadcom's custom AI ASIC business (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) is partly a hedge for hyperscalers against Nvidia dominance. If Nvidia's TAM expands via restored China access, the relative competitive narrative for custom silicon softens at the margin.

Intel's Gaudi accelerators were positioned partly as a China-compliant alternative. If Nvidia regains direct China access with competitive parts, Intel's already-weak AI accelerator pitch in China weakens further.

headlines: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang got the last Air Force One seat to China. Why it’s so crucial for the chipmaker.

macro_events.event_summary: Silver is being repriced alongside copper as an AI infrastructure build-out commodity, with industrial demand from data centers, power electronics, and solar driving the narrative.
primary_drivers: Industrial silver demand from AI data center power electronics and solar PV
Momentum/rotation into precious-industrial metals as AI capex proxies
Tight physical silver market with structural deficit narrative

beneficiaries.rationale: Pan American Silver — large-cap pure-play primary silver miner with high revenue leverage to silver price; standard vehicle for institutional silver exposure.
First Majestic Silver — among the highest silver revenue concentration (~50%+) of major listed miners; high beta to spot silver moves, common momentum vehicle on silver narrative days.
Hecla Mining — largest US-based primary silver producer; benefits from both silver price and 'US domestic critical minerals' framing tied to AI build-out.
Wheaton Precious Metals — streaming company with major silver stream exposure and operating leverage to silver price without single-mine risk; institutional preferred vehicle.
Freeport-McMoRan — already-established 'AI copper' name explicitly referenced as the peer silver is joining; reflexively bid as the trade broadens and gets re-flagged in media.
MP Materials — adjacent 'critical minerals for AI build-out' beneficiary; tends to rotate with copper/silver on AI-infrastructure-commodity theme days even without direct silver exposure.

headlines: Silver is joining copper in the AI build-out trade: Chart of the Day

macro_events.event_summary: U.S. Treasury yields declined while the dollar firmed — a mixed signal with no clear catalyst named in the headline.
primary_drivers: Lower discount rate from falling yields supports long-duration assets
Stronger dollar pressures multinationals with foreign revenue and commodity exporters
headlines: U.S. Treasury Yields Decline, Dollar Firms

macro_events.event_summary: Conventional mortgage and refinance rates ticked up across the board on May 13, 2026, modestly raising borrowing costs for homebuyers and refinancers.
primary_drivers: Higher mortgage rates reduce home affordability and refi volume
Net interest margin tailwind for mortgage-rate-sensitive lenders is offset by lower origination volume
Routine daily rate move — not a regime change
headlines: Mortgage and refinance interest rates today, May 13, 2026: Conventional rates up across the board
